title: 396Hz Sitar, Flute, Tabla for Late Night Anxiety Reset | Darbari Kanada
hook_angle: Late-night emotional acceptance and letting go
hook_phrase: You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here.
music_prompt:
Instruments: Sitar, Bansuri Flute, and Tabla. Raga: Darbari Kanada. BPM: 45 BPM.
Each note must decay with a long, natural reverb. Ensure wide silence between musical phrases, emphasizing the contemplative nature of the raga. There should be no buildup, no climax, maintaining a consistent, deeply meditative texture throughout. Record with a warm, analog quality. The structure must be loop-friendly and seamless.
Sitar will lead with a weighty and introspective alap, exploring the deep komal rishab and komal gandhar notes with heavy meend (glides). The bansuri flute will answer the sitar's phrases with its own delicate, midnight-infused expressions. Tabla will enter softly and subtly at approximately 3 minutes, playing a very slow and gentle dadra taal.
The playing feel should evoke majestic stillness, allowing thoughts to settle like sediment, creating a profound late-night depth.
Explicitly exclude: No Western instruments. No aggressive percussion fills. No sudden dynamic shifts or abrupt changes in intensity.
image_prompt:
Madhubani folk painting style from Mithila, Bihar, India. 16:9 widescreen format. Flat 2D perspective with bold hand-drawn black outlines. Hand-painted texture on aged parchment or natural fabric. Vibrant natural pigments: deep red, saffron yellow, peacock teal, forest green, ivory white, turmeric gold. Characteristic nature motifs: peacocks, lotus flowers, mango leaves, fish, deer, birds. Intricate geometric and floral borders with elephants and paisley.
Scene: A serene night garden where three musicians are seated under a large, flowering tree - a sitar player, a bansuri flute player, and a tabla player. A full moon glows softly overhead, depicted as a stylized mandala. Peacocks and deer are shown listening attentively in the shadows of the garden foliage. Several small oil lamps (diyas) are arranged in a gentle semicircle around the musicians. The overall palette should be dominated by deep indigo for the night sky, contrasted with rich golds and silvers for the moon, stars, and lamp glow. The stylized sky is filled with delicate stars.
No text or letters anywhere. Pure illustration only. No watermarks.
thumbnail_tagline: Saturday Night Deep Relaxation for the Soul
description:
You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here.

Welcome to DhunDetox, your dedicated sanctuary for mental wellness through the timeless beauty of Indian classical music. This unique composition features the profound Raga Darbari Kanada, an ancient melody beautifully rendered with the soulful Sitar, the ethereal Bansuri Flute, and the grounding Tabla. Tuned precisely to a calming 396Hz frequency, known for its ability to release fear and guilt, this video is thoughtfully designed for urban professionals aged 25-40. It offers a much-needed reset for late-night anxiety, guiding you towards profound relaxation and inner peace after a demanding day. Let the intricate yet soothing ancient melodies gently wash over you, recentering your spirit and bringing a quietude to your restless mind.

Use this deeply resonant music for:
*   Deep meditation & mindfulness practice
*   Calm and restorative sleep
*   Gentle yoga & stretching sessions
*   Focused study & concentrated work
*   Effective anxiety & stress relief
*   Evening reflection & introspection
*   Unwinding after a long and busy day
*   Promoting mental clarity and emotional balance
*   Creating a peaceful ambient atmosphere
*   Cultivating overall well-being

Raga Darbari Kanada, with its majestic, contemplative, and late-night gravity, is renowned for its ability to instill a sense of profound calm and introspection. Its deep, resonant notes are perfect for quieting a restless mind, offering solace and an anchor in moments of emotional turmoil, making it ideal for a late-night anxiety reset.

यह भारतीय शास्त्रीय संगीत वीडियो रात की चिंता और तनाव से मुक्ति के लिए बनाया गया है। सितार, बांसुरी और तबला के मधुर स्वर, राग दरबारी कानाड़ा में, आपके मन को शांति और सुकून देंगे। 396Hz की आवृत्ति आपको गहरी नींद और मानसिक शांति प्रदान करेगी।

title: When Your Mind Needs Peace 🌅 Raag Bhairavi | Bansuri & Tanpura for Sunday Reset
description:
🌅 When Your Mind Needs Peace 🌅 Raag Bhairavi | Bansuri & Tanpura for Sunday Reset

You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here. This Sunday morning, allow the gentle, devotional strains of Raag Bhairavi to wash away the burdens of the past week. Surrender to its profound emotional depth and prepare your mind for a fresh beginning.

This deeply meditative composition in **Raag Bhairavi** blends the soulful **bansuri flute & tanpura** to support your morning meditation, emotional release, and mental clarity, preparing you for a week filled with peace and purpose.

> *"You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here."*

Raag Bhairavi, often called the "Mother of all Raags," is celebrated for its deeply devotional and melancholic character. Traditionally sung at dawn, its unique blend of all-komal (flat) notes creates a profound sense of surrender, introspection, and emotional completion. It gently nudges the listener towards acceptance, making it perfect for releasing tension and embracing inner peace.

**🌅 Perfect for:**
- Morning Meditation
- Sunday Reset & Recharge
- Washing away week's stress
- Cultivating emotional surrender
- Achieving mental clarity
- Preparing for a productive week
- Deep introspection
- Anxiety relief at dawn

**🎵 Why Raag Bhairavi heals:**
Raag Bhairavi, with its profound komal notes, is renowned for its ability to activate the parasympathetic nervous system. It gently guides the mind into a state of deep surrender and acceptance, releasing emotional tension and fostering profound inner peace, helping you feel grounded and refreshed.

**🎧 How to use this music:**
1. Use headphones at a comfortable, low volume
2. Dim the lights or turn them off completely
3. Lie down or sit comfortably with eyes closed
4. Take 3 slow deep breaths — exhale longer than you inhale
5. Let the bansuri flute guide your thoughts into softness
6. Let the tanpura slow your breath
7. Let the soundscape ground you in peace
8. Stay for at least 15–20 minutes for the full effect

---

**🇮🇳 हिंदी में:**
यह रविवार सुबह, राग भैरवी की शांत, भक्तिपूर्ण धुनें आपके मन को शांत करेंगी। बांसुरी और तानपुरा का यह संयोजन सुबह के ध्यान, भावनात्मक शांति और मानसिक स्पष्टता के लिए आदर्श है।
*"आपको अभी ठीक होने की ज़रूरत नहीं है। बस यहीं रहें।"*

---

💛 If this music brought you stillness tonight, please **LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and SHARE** with someone who needs peace.
🔔 **Subscribe to DhunDetox** for more healing frequencies, Indian classical meditation music, raga therapy, and mindful sound journeys.

music_prompt: A deeply meditative composition featuring Bansuri flute and a continuous Tanpura drone in Raag Bhairavi. Bansuri plays warm and intimate phrases, emphasizing meend glides and Bhairavi's characteristic komal (flat) notes to create profound emotional depth and a sense of surrender. The overall style is alap-like, with wide silences between phrases. Each note decays with long reverb. The recording should possess a warm analog quality, be loop-friendly, and maintain a deeply meditative atmosphere with no buildup or climax. title: When Your Thoughts Drift 💡 Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Laser Focus
description:
💡 When Your Thoughts Drift 💡 Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Laser Focus

Is your mind a whirlwind of distractions? This powerful sound journey is designed to anchor your thoughts, bringing you into a state of unwavering concentration, perfect for deep work or study.

This deeply meditative composition in **Raag Chandrakauns** blends the healing resonance of **741Hz** — known for awakening intuition and promoting problem-solving — with soulful **veena & tabla** to enhance concentration and clarity.

> *"For the next 2 hours, nothing is your problem."*

Raag Chandrakauns, a rare and profound melody, is often associated with the quiet intensity of midnight, evoking a sense of deep introspection and mysterious contemplation. Its unique scale is known to calm the nervous system, reduce mental chatter, and cultivate profound inner stillness, creating an ideal environment for focused thought.

**💡 Perfect for:**
- Dissolving mental fog
- Achieving deep work states
- Enhancing problem-solving skills
- Boosting creative flow
- Mindful study sessions
- Pre-exam anxiety relief
- Focusing on complex tasks
- Quieting an overactive mind

**🎵 Why Raag Chandrakauns heals:**
The unique, austere notes of Chandrakauns gently guide your brain into the parasympathetic state, easing nervous tension and sharpening cognitive function. Combined with the **741Hz** frequency, which supports clarity and intuitive thinking, this raga-frequency combination becomes a powerful tool for mental discipline and sustained focus, aligning your mind with the task at hand.

**🎧 How to use this music:**
1. Use headphones at a comfortable, low volume
2. Dim the lights or turn them off completely
3. Lie down or sit comfortably with eyes closed
4. Take 3 slow deep breaths — exhale longer than you inhale
5. Let the veena's haunting melody guide your thoughts into softness
6. Let the tabla's sparse rhythm slow your breath
7. Let the veena's resonating strings ground you in peace
8. Stay for at least 15–20 minutes for the full effect

---

**🇮🇳 हिंदी में:**
क्या आपके विचार भटक रहे हैं? यह ध्यानपूर्ण रचना आपको गहन एकाग्रता की स्थिति में लाने के लिए बनाई गई है। राग चंद्रकौंस और 741Hz की वीणा और तबला की ध्वनि आपके मन को शांति देगी और उसे केंद्रित करेगी।
*"अपने अगले 2 घंटे के लिए, कोई समस्या नहीं है, बस यहां रहें।"*

---

title: When the Day Won't Let Go 😌 Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar & Flute for Stress Detox
description:
😌 When the Day Won't Let Go 😌 Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar & Flute for Stress Detox

Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem. Lower your cortisol. Slow your breath. Let this raga do the rest.

This deeply meditative composition in Raag Yaman Kalyan blends the healing resonance of 174Hz — known for reducing physical pain and tension, promoting a sense of security and well-being — with soulful sitar & bansuri flute to help your body unclenching, your mind quiet, and the city noise dissolving into a gentle calm.

"Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem."

Raag Yaman Kalyan is traditionally sung at dusk, capturing the transition from the day's activity to the stillness of night. Its gentle, romantic notes are known to soothe the mind, ease the heart, and prepare the body for restful repose. This ancient melody guides your nervous system into a state of deep calm, allowing the accumulated tension of the day to simply melt away.

😌 PERFECT FOR:
- Evening Cortisol Drop
- Stress Relief
- Unclenching the Body
- Dissolving City Noise
- Deep Relaxation
- Preparing for Sleep
- Nervous System Calm
- Mind Reset after work

🎵 WHY RAAG YAMAN KALYAN HEALS:
Yaman Kalyan's unique melodic structure and the gentle vibrations of 174Hz actively stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system, shifting your body from 'fight or flight' to 'rest and digest.' This facilitates deep physical and mental restoration, fostering inner peace and emotional balance. Its specific scale and movement are designed to evoke tranquility and a sense of gentle surrender.

🎧 HOW TO USE THIS MUSIC:
1. Use headphones at a comfortable, low volume
2. Dim the lights or turn them off completely
3. Lie down or sit comfortably with eyes closed
4. Take 3 slow deep breaths — exhale longer than you inhale
5. Let the sitar guide your thoughts into softness
6. Let the bansuri slow your breath
7. Let the melody ground you in peace
8. Stay for at least 15–20 minutes for the full effect

────────────────────────────

🇮🇳 हिंदी में:
दिन भर की थकान उतारने और तनाव से मुक्ति पाने के लिए यह राग यमन कल्याण सितार और बांसुरी की धुन। 174Hz की शांत आवृत्ति आपके शरीर को आराम देगी और मन को शांति प्रदान करेगी। शाम के समय कॉर्टिसोल के स्तर को कम करने और गहरी नींद के लिए आदर्श।
"दुनिया को एक तरफ रख दें। फिलहाल, आपकी कोई समस्या नहीं है।"

────────────────────────────

title: When Overthinking Won't Quiet 🌧️ Raag Bageshri 528Hz | Bansuri for Anxiety Relief
description:
🌧️ When Overthinking Won't Quiet 🌧️ Raag Bageshri 528Hz | Bansuri for Anxiety Relief

Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem. You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here.

This deeply meditative composition in Raag Bageshri blends the healing resonance of 528Hz — known for its transformative power and ability to restore inner balance — with soulful bansuri flute to quiet the overthinking mind, release anxiety, and gently guide you towards deep inner stillness.

"Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem. You don't need to be okay right now. Just be here."

Raag Bageshri, created by the legendary Mian Tansen, is traditionally performed late at night, evoking a profound sense of introspection and gentle longing. It's the perfect soundscape for emotions to quietly surface and release, allowing you to shed the burden of held thoughts and find solace in the present moment.

🧘‍♀️ PERFECT FOR:
- Stop Overthinking
- Anxiety Reset
- Deep Inner Stillness
- Emotional Detox
- Nervous System Calm
- Mind Reset
- Late Night Reflection
- Quiet the Mind Before Sleep

🎵 WHY RAAG BAGESHRI HEALS:
Raag Bageshri, played in its vilambit (slow) tempo, directly engages the parasympathetic nervous system, inducing a profound state of rest and digest. Its characteristic komal (soft) notes and introspective mood allow for emotional release, while the 528Hz frequency amplifies this healing by promoting cellular relaxation and a sense of profound well-being.

🎧 HOW TO USE THIS MUSIC:
1. Use headphones at a comfortable, low volume
2. Dim the lights or turn them off completely
3. Lie down or sit comfortably with eyes closed
4. Take 3 slow deep breaths — exhale longer than you inhale
5. Let the bansuri flute guide your thoughts into softness
6. Let the tanpura drone slow your breath
7. Let the subtle tabla ground you in peace
8. Stay for at least 15–20 minutes for the full effect

────────────────────────────

🇮🇳 हिंदी में:
बारिश की शांत रातों में, जब मन बेचैन हो। यह राग बागेश्री 528Hz में बांसुरी की धुन आपके अत्यधिक विचारों को शांत करेगी और चिंता से राहत देगी। अपनी साँसों को ढीला छोड़ दें और संगीत को आपको भीतर तक शांत करने दें।
"दुनिया को नीचे रख दो। अभी, कुछ भी तुम्हारी समस्या नहीं है। तुम्हें अभी ठीक होने की ज़रूरत नहीं है। बस यहाँ रहो।"

────────────────────────────

music_prompt:
Solo bansuri, breathy and intimate, plays Raag Bageshri in vilambit (slow) tempo at 55 BPM. "Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem."

title: When Your Morning Needs a Reset ☀️ Raag Bhupali 432Hz | Sitar, Sarod & Tabla for Prana Flow

It's known for clearing the mind and promoting inner stillness, making it an ideal companion for your morning rituals to reduce cortisol and foster a sense of grounded tranquility.

title: When the New Week Feels Heavy 🌅 Raag Bhairavi 432Hz | Bansuri for Morning Cortisol
description:
🌅 When the New Week Feels Heavy 🌅 Raag Bhairavi 432Hz | Bansuri for Morning Cortisol

Lower your cortisol. Slow your breath. Let this raga do the rest. Before the world begins, meet yourself in stillness.

This deeply meditative composition in Raag Bhairavi blends the healing resonance of 432Hz — known for its natural tuning that resonates with the human body and promotes a sense of calm and well-being — with soulful bansuri flute & tanpura to facilitate a morning cortisol reset and profound meditation.

"Lower your cortisol. Slow your breath. Let this raga do the rest."

Raag Bhairavi, often described as the most emotionally complete raga, is traditionally performed at dawn, invoking devotion, surrender, and deep melancholic beauty. Its tender, all-komal (flat) notes gently guide the nervous system into a parasympathetic state, helping to soothe the mind and prepare for the week ahead with a sense of grounded acceptance and inner peace.

🌅 PERFECT FOR:
- Morning cortisol reset
- Starting the new week with calm
- Pre-work meditation
- Releasing new week dread
- Surrender to daily challenges
- Emotional detox and clarity
- Grounding before demands
- Gentle, mindful awakening

🎵 WHY RAAG BHAIRAVI HEALS:
Bhairavi's ancient melodic structure, delivered through the soft, ethereal bansuri over the steady tanpura drone, directly impacts the parasympathetic nervous system, encouraging deep relaxation. The 432Hz frequency further enhances this effect, aligning you with natural rhythms and fostering a profound sense of inner stillness and emotional release.

🎧 HOW TO USE THIS MUSIC:
1. Use headphones at a comfortable, low volume
2. Dim the lights or turn them off completely
3. Lie down or sit comfortably with eyes closed
4. Take 3 slow deep breaths — exhale longer than you inhale
5. Let the bansuri flute guide your thoughts into softness
6. Let the tanpura slow your breath
7. Stay for at least 15–20 minutes for the full effect

────────────────────────────

यह ध्यानपूर्ण संगीत, 432Hz की सुखदायक आवृत्ति पर राग भैरवी में, आपकी सुबह की शुरुआत में शांति लाता है। बांसुरी और तानपुरा के साथ, यह तनाव कम करने और नए सप्ताह के लिए खुद को तैयार करने में मदद करता है।
"अपने कॉर्टिसोल को कम करें। अपनी सांस धीमी करें। बाकी काम इस राग को करने दें।"

hook_phrase: Lower your cortisol. Slow your breath. Let this raga do the rest.
music_prompt: Compose a deeply meditative piece in Raag Bhairavi featuring bansuri flute and a continuous tanpura drone. The BPM should be slow and steady, around 40-45, maintaining a vilambit (slow) to madhya laya (medium tempo) progression, but always deeply unhurried. The bansuri should play with a warm, intimate, and ethereal tone, emphasizing Bhairavi's profound emotional depth through long, expressive meend glides and tender articulation of its characteristic all-komal notes. Each bansuri phrase should decay with a long, natural reverb, followed by wide, contemplative silences. The composition should be in an alap (unaccompanied, free-rhythm introduction) style, with no rhythmic cycles or sudden dynamic changes, focusing purely on melodic exploration and emotional surrender. Ensure a warm, analog recording quality, making the structure loop-friendly for extended meditation. The mood should evoke a sense of washing clean, openness, and readiness for the new week. Explicitly exclude any Western instruments, percussion fills, or sudden dynamic shifts. The tanpura drone should be a constant, unwavering presence, providing a grounded foundation for the bansuri's soaring melodies.

The scene depicts a serene bansuri flute and tanpura player, a woman, seated cross-legged at the gently lapping edge of the Ganges River at dawn. Her eyes are closed in deep meditation, a tranquil expression on her face, adorned in an ornate saffron and ivory sari. A finely crafted tanpura rests beside her on a lotus-patterned mat. The sky above is a canvas of warm saffron and golden hues, transitioning to soft ivory, with a stylized, radiant rising sun in the distance, emanating concentric golden rings. Birds, possibly white cranes or herons, are seen flying in graceful formation across the awakening sky. The water of the Ganges is dotted with blooming lotus flowers, their petals unfurling in shades of soft pink and ivory, reflecting the golden light. Visible sound ripples, like soft golden energy waves, emanate from the bansuri, gently touching the lotus flowers and diffusing into the tranquil dawn air. A warm golden glow envelops the musician, creating a soft halo effect. The entire composition is framed by an ornate, hand-painted botanical border featuring entwined lotus vines and a subtle geometric fish-and-wave motif, rendered with exquisite crosshatching and stippling on a textured, aged parchment background. The overall palette is dominated by saffron, warm gold, ivory, and fresh greens, capturing the profound calm and spiritual essence of a new beginning by the sacred river.
